sections.heading: AI Features in Data Explorer
sections.body:
The [intelligent assistant](de-ai-assistant) is an AI-powered tool designed for
integrated, expert MongoDB development support in the Atlas UI.

You can also [Query with Natural Language](atlas-ui-query-natural-language)
in MongoDB Atlas. MongoDB Atlas uses AI to generate filter queries and
aggregations based on the prompts you provide.

sections.heading: Read-only Tools
sections.body:
When using [Enable read-only tools in the MongoDB Assistant](de-read-only-tools) in the intelligent
assistant, the assistant may access information about the current query or
aggregation or any data in the database itself via a tool call, but only with
explicit user approval.

sections.heading: Third Party Providers
sections.body:
AI features in Atlas currently use the
[Azure OpenAI Service](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/ai-services/openai-service)
hosted by Microsoft.  This is subject to change in the future.

sections.heading: How Your Data is Used
sections.body:
When you use AI features in Atlas, the following information may be sent
to MongoDB's backend and/or the third-party AI provider:

For [Query with Natural Language](atlas-ui-query-natural-language):

- The full text of your natural language prompt.

- The schema of the collection you are querying,
  including database name, collection name, field names, and types.

- Sample field values to improve the quality of recommendations.

  > **Note:** You can prevent Atlas from sending sample field values in the
  > [project-level settings](atlas-ui-ai-features-project-level-disable).
  > If you disable this setting, you can still use natural language querying,
  > however, responses might be less accurate.

For the [intelligent assistant](de-ai-assistant):

- Explain plan metadata. The **Interpret** button uses Explain plan metadata,
  including the original query, to process requests. The assistant prompts a
  user to confirm that they're okay with this before proceeding with the request
  each time.

- Sanitized connection information. The **Debug** button passes sanitized
  connection information and error messages to the assistant.

- The tab you are working on in Atlas.

- Database tool calling sends additional data to the AI provider, depending on
  which tool is executed. All tools require explicit user approval. See the full
  list of [Enable read-only tools in the MongoDB Assistant](de-read-only-tools) for an overview of
  data used.

The information that is sent will not be shared with any other third
parties or stored by the AI provider. We do not send unsanitized
connection strings, credentials, rows, or documents from your databases.

sections.heading: Disable AI Features
sections.body: You can disable AI-powered features at the project and organization levels.

sections.heading: Don't Share Sample Field Values
sections.body:
If you want to use AI features in Data Explorer but want to prevent
Atlas from sending sample field values to MongoDB or third-party
providers, you can opt out from sharing field values within a project.> **Note:** If you disable this functionality, responses from your natural language
> queries and other AI features might be less accurate.**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo opt out from sharing field values with the Atlas Administration API,
send a `PATCH` request to the [settings](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateprojectsettings) endpoint with the
`isDataExplorerGenAISampleDocumentPassingEnabled` boolean flag value set
to `false`.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo opt out from sharing field values with the Atlas CLI, run
the following command:```
atlas projects settings update --disableDataExplorerGenAISampleDocumentPassing
```**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Don't share sample field values.
sections.body:
Set the
**Enable Sending Sample Field Values in Data Explorer Generative AI Features**
toggle to **off**.

sections.heading: Disable Natural Language Querying
sections.body:
If you no longer want to use natural language querying on your project, you can
turn this feature off in the project-level settings.**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo disable natural language querying with the Atlas Administration API,
send a `PATCH` request to the [settings](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateprojectsettings) endpoint with the
`isDataExplorerGenAIFeaturesEnabled` boolean flag value set to
`false`.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo disable natural language querying with the Atlas CLI, run the
following command:```
atlas projects settings update --disableDataExplorerGenAIFeatures
```**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Disable natural language querying.
sections.body:
Set the **Enable Data Explorer Generative AI Features**
toggle to **off**.

sections.heading: Disable All AI Features on Atlas
sections.body:
If you want to prevent users in your organization from using all Atlas
features that use generative AI, you can change this setting at the organization
level.**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo disable AI features for an organization with the Atlas Administration API,
send a `PATCH` request to the [settings](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateorganization) endpoint with the
`genAIFeaturesEnabled` boolean flag value set to `false`.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo disable AI features for an organization with the Atlas CLI, specify
`genAIFeaturesEnabled: false` in a JSON configuration file:```json
{
   "genAIFeaturesEnabled": false
}
```After you update the `genAIFeaturesEnabled` field in your configuration
file, run the following command to update the organization settings:```
atlas api updateOrgSettings --file <path-to-config-file>
```**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Disable the AI feature.
sections.body:
Set the **Enable Atlas features that use generative AI**
toggle to **off**.

sections.heading: Disable Read-Only Tools on Atlas
sections.body:
If you no longer want to use read-only tools on your project, you can
turn this feature off in the project-level settings.**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo disable read-only tools with the Atlas Administration API,
send a `PATCH` request to the [settings
\<updateprojectsettings>\`endpoint with the
\`\`enableGenAIToolCalling\`](<https://www.mongodb.com/docs/api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-settings
\<updateprojectsettings\>`endpoint with the
``enableGenAIToolCalling`>) boolean flag value set to
`false`.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo disable read-only tools for a project with the Atlas CLI,
specify `enableGenAIToolCalling: false` in a JSON configuration file:```json
{
   "enableGenAIToolCalling": false
}
```After you update the `enableGenAIToolCalling` field in your configuration
file, run the following command to update the project settings:```
atlas api projects updateGroupSettings --file <path-to-config-file>
```**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Disable Tool Calling
sections.body:
Disable [Enable read-only tools in the MongoDB Assistant](de-read-only-tools)
by toggling **Enable read-only tools in the MongoDB Assistant**.
If you disable tool calling at the project label, you will be unable to
re-enable it from the data explorer interface.
